# The Impact of Fake Reviews on Demand and Welfare

**Authors:** Jesper Akesson, Robert W. Hahn, Robert D. Metcalfe, Manuel Monti-Nussbaum  
**Year:** 2023  
**URL:** https://www.nber.org/papers/w31836  
**Evidence type:** Incentive-compatible online experiment

## Claim supported

Positive fake reviews can cause consumers to choose lower-quality products and reduce welfare.

## Key result

Experiment with 10,000 UK participants; welfare losses estimated around $0.12 per dollar spent in the studied setting.

## How it supports manufactured traction

This source strengthens the argument that digital systems can be moved by manipulated proxy signals. Use it as evidence for the mechanism, not as proof that every campaign succeeds.
